title: Redis systemd Service Unit
summary: A Linux systemd unit for running Redis in the foreground with automatic restarts, file descriptor limits, and an example 8 GB memory cap.
verification_setup: Install Redis with a redis-server binary and systemd support.
Create the redis user and group if your distribution does not provide them.
Adjust the ExecStart binary path, redis.conf path, and MemoryMax value for your distribution before verification.
verification_command: Manual verification only; see README.md for the systemd verification checklist.
verification_notes: Not executed. The unit is adapted from the official Redis 7.4 systemd example, but it still requires validation on a Linux host with systemd and a Redis build that supports systemd supervision.
